title: Tool return type (String, bool) is stringly-typed — replace with Result<ToolOutput, ToolError> enum
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Architectural Alignment / Error Orchestration / Type Safety

**Location:** pervasive across /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/ — every tool returns (String, bool). Examples: bash/mod.rs:309, file/read.rs:40, file/write.rs:9, file/edit.rs:9, chainlink.rs:11, lsp.rs:73, web.rs:9, worktree.rs:59, cron.rs:143, task.rs:7, ask_user.rs:8.

**Violation:**
Every tool function returns (String, bool) — the first element is both success text AND error text depending on the second bool. This violates:
1. **Type safety.** There is no way for the type system to prevent swapping the two components. A refactor that flips the bool leaks errors as success. No compile-time guarantee that errors carry error data and not arbitrary text.
2. **Loss of structure.** The caller (check_tool_result_marker, tool_intercept.rs format_tool_results_xml_with_names) regex-greps for 'error' / 'Error' / 'failed' to decide completion hints (tool_intercept.rs:718) — a classic stringly-typed anti-pattern.
3. **Error category is erased.** The caller cannot distinguish 'permission denied' from 'file not found' from 'upstream 500' without substring matching.
4. **Markers are stuffed into the same String.** USER_QUESTION_MARKER, ENTER_PLAN_MODE_MARKER are detected by JSON-parsing the content (mod.rs:923) — a brittle in-band signalling.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Define:
   pub enum ToolError { InvalidArg(String), PermissionDenied(String), NotFound(String), Io(io::Error), Timeout, Upstream(String), Policy(String) }
   pub enum ToolOutput { Text(String), Image { mime: String, data: Vec<u8> }, Structured(serde_json::Value), Marker(ToolMarker) }
   pub enum ToolMarker { UserQuestion(Vec<Question>), EnterPlanMode, ExitPlanMode { allowed: Vec<AllowedPrompt> } }
2. Every execute_* function returns Result<ToolOutput, ToolError>. Callers pattern-match the marker branch directly.
3. Delete check_tool_result_marker, parse_user_questions, parse_exit_plan_mode_prompts — the Marker variant IS the marker.
4. format_tool_results_xml_with_names' completion hints become methods on ToolOutput (ToolOutput::completion_hint(&self) -> Option<&str>) — no string matching.
